mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated Google Cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
The Google Cloud SDK provides the gcloud command-line tool for managing GKE and other Google Cloud resources.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> This command installs the Google Cloud SDK.
> Reload the shell to update the environment.
> Initialize the SDK and authenticate with your Google account.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> Ensure you replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the GKE cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features such as autoscaling and monitoring for your GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

```

> This enables cluster autoscaling.
> This enables monitoring with Stackdriver.
commands: gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --master --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --node-pool default-pool --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

kubectl get nodes

```

> Upgrade the master node.
> Upgrade the node pool.
> Verify that all nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.
commands: gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --master --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --node-pool default-pool --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not running or kubeconfig is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions for operation'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure your account has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role assigned.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node is in a NotReady state'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is not properly initialized or has resource issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs with 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified cluster name is incorrect or does not exist.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name with 'gcloud container clusters list'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node types and improved integration with Anthos.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for certain resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to review the new node types and adjust configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31
gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and your Google Cloud account set up.
